content: If we map the iterative self-feedback loop of Self-Refine (for static output refinement) onto Reflexion’s dynamic long-term memory for autonomous agents, we can build an agent that not only reflects on individual task outputs, but retroactively refines its stored past memory episodes to improve future decision-making across sequential tasks. This creates a compounding self-improvement cycle that avoids the common pitfall of Reflexion agents accumulating flawed or unhelpful cached memories over time.
